sentence: O gato gris non está no tellado hoxe.
ipa: o ˈhato ˈhɾis ˈnoŋ eˈsta no teˈʎaðo ˈoʃe
gloss_en: The grey cat is not on the roof today.
notes: Gheada: /ɡ/ realised as a voiceless fricative [h] in onset 'gato' [hato] and before a liquid 'gris' [hɾis] — the defining western reflex; palatal lateral ⟨ll⟩ [ʎ] 'tellado'; coda [ŋ] 'non' per fernandez_rei1990.

sentence: Estes zapatos custan corenta euros.
ipa: ˈestes θaˈpatos ˈkustaŋ koˈɾenta ˈewɾos
gloss_en: These shoes cost forty euros.
notes: Distinción retained alongside gheada: ⟨z⟩ [θ] 'zapatos' — this spec models gheada, not the coastal seseo; coda [ŋ] and ⟨s⟩+voiced sandhi per fernandez_rei1990.

sentence: A xuntanza cos amigos é mañá ás tres.
ipa: a ʃuˈntanθa kos aˈmihos ˈɛ maˈɲa ˈas ˈtɾes
gloss_en: The gathering with the friends is tomorrow at three.
notes: Gheada intervocalic 'amigos' [amihos]; open-mid [ɛ] 'é'; palatal nasal ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ] 'mañá' per fernandez_rei1990.

sentence: Meu irmán mergullouse na auga fría.
ipa: ˈmew iˈɾmaŋ meˈɾɡʎowse na ˈawha ˈfɾia
gloss_en: My brother plunged into the cold water.
notes: Gheada intervocalic 'auga' [awha]; palatal lateral ⟨ll⟩ [ʎ] 'mergullouse'; word-final velar [ŋ] 'irmán' per fernandez_rei1990.

sentence: Vai de fronte e garda a grade no galpón.
ipa: ˈbaj de ˈfɾonte e ˈhaɾda a ˈhɾaðe no haˈlpɔŋ
gloss_en: Go straight ahead and store the harrow in the shed.
notes: Gheada across onsets 'garda' [haɾda], 'grade' [hɾaðe], 'galpón' [halpɔŋ]; open-mid [ɔ] and coda [ŋ] 'galpón' per fernandez_rei1990.

sentence: A caldeirada daquel lugar estaba moi boa.
ipa: a kaldeˈjɾaða daˈkel luˈhaɾ eˈstaβa ˈmoj ˈboa
gloss_en: The fish stew at that place was very good.
notes: Gheada intervocalic 'lugar' [luhaɾ]; regional lexicon 'caldeirada' per fernandez_rei1990.

sentence: Onde gardas a chave do garaxe?
ipa: ˈonde ˈhaɾdas a ˈtʃabe do haˈɾaʃe
gloss_en: Where do you keep the garage key?
notes: Gheada onset 'gardas' [haɾdas], 'garaxe' [haɾaʃe]; ⟨x⟩ [ʃ] 'garaxe' per fernandez_rei1990.

sentence: Cantos gatos grandes teñen na granxa?
ipa: ˈkantos ˈhatos ˈhɾandes ˈteɲeŋ na ˈhɾanʃa
gloss_en: How many big cats do they have on the farm?
notes: Gheada 'gatos'/'grandes'/'granxa' [hatos/hɾandes/hɾanʃa]; palatal nasal 'teñen' [teɲeŋ] with a velar coda per fernandez_rei1990.

sentence: Se eu pagase, mercaría aquela galiña gorda.
ipa: se ˈew paˈhase meɾkaˈɾia aˈkela haˈliɲa ˈhoɾda
gloss_en: If I paid, I would buy that fat hen.
notes: Gheada 'pagase'/'galiña'/'gorda'; palatal nasal ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ] 'galiña' per fernandez_rei1990.

sentence: Onte merquei cinco quilos de cereixas na praza.
ipa: ˈonte ˈmeɾkej ˈθinko ˈkilos de θeˈɾejʃas na ˈpɾaθa
gloss_en: Yesterday I bought five kilos of cherries in the square.
notes: Distinción ⟨c/z⟩ [θ] 'cinco'/'cereixas'/'praza' — the coastal seseo merger is not modelled here, so /θ/ stays per freixeiro_mato1998.

sentence: O ano que vén hei viaxar a Vigo.
ipa: o ˈano ke ˈbɛŋ ˈej bjaˈʃaɾ a ˈbiho
gloss_en: Next year I will travel to Vigo.
notes: Gheada in the place-name 'Vigo' [biho]; open-mid [ɛ] 'vén'; ⟨x⟩ [ʃ] 'viaxar' per fernandez_rei1990.

sentence: Chámame cando chegues ó regato.
ipa: ˈtʃamame ˈkando ˈtʃeɡes ˈɔ reˈhato
gloss_en: Call me when you reach the stream.
notes: Gheada 'regato' [rehato]; open-mid [ɔ] 'ó'; word-initial trill [r] 'regato' per fernandez_rei1990.

sentence: Boas tardes, gústache o galego da bisbarra?
ipa: ˈboas ˈtaɾdes ˈhustatʃe o haˈleho da biˈsbara
gloss_en: Good afternoon, do you like the Galician of the district?
notes: Gheada 'gústache'/'galego' [hustatʃe/haleho]; trill [r] 'bisbarra' per fernandez_rei1990.

sentence: Máis vale paxaro na man que cento voando.
ipa: ˈmais ˈbale paˈʃaɾo na ˈmaŋ ke ˈθento ˈboando
gloss_en: A bird in the hand is worth two in the bush.
notes: ⟨x⟩ [ʃ] 'paxaro' and distinción ⟨ce⟩ [θ] 'cento'; word-final velar [ŋ] 'man' per freixeiro_mato1998.

sentence: O can gordo corre pola ribeira do río.
ipa: o ˈkaŋ ˈhoɾdo ˈkɔre ˈpɔla riˈβejɾa do ˈrio
gloss_en: The fat dog runs along the riverbank.
notes: Gheada 'gordo' [hoɾdo]; word-final velar [ŋ] 'can'; trill 'corre'/'río' per fernandez_rei1990.

sentence: A muller vella colle a agulla amarela.
ipa: a muˈʎeɾ ˈbeʎa ˈkoʎe a ˈaɡʎa amaˈɾela
gloss_en: The old woman picks up the yellow needle.
notes: Palatal lateral ⟨ll⟩ [ʎ] 'muller'/'vella'/'colle'/'agulla' (no yeísmo) per freixeiro_mato1998.

sentence: O café da esquina é moi forte.
ipa: o kaˈfɛ da eˈskina ˈɛ ˈmoj ˈfoɾte
gloss_en: The coffee from the corner is very strong.
notes: Open-mid [ɛ] 'café'/'é'; hiatus 'esquina é' per freixeiro_mato1998.

sentence: A viña da montaña dá bo viño.
ipa: a ˈbiɲa da moˈntaɲa ˈda ˈbo ˈbiɲo
gloss_en: The vineyard on the mountain gives good wine.
notes: Palatal nasal ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ] 'viña'/'montaña'/'viño' per freixeiro_mato1998.

sentence: Unha das gaivotas gañou algunha migalla.
ipa: uŋa das haˈjbotas ˈhaɲow ˈalɡŋa miˈhaʎa
gloss_en: One of the gulls won some crumb.
notes: Gheada 'gaivotas'/'gañou' [hajbotas/haɲow]; palatal nasal 'gañou' [ɲ]; velar nasal ⟨nh⟩ [ŋ] 'unha' per fernandez_rei1990.

sentence: A auga do rego move o gran muíño.
ipa: a ˈawha do ˈreho ˈmobe o ˈhɾaŋ ˈmuiɲo
gloss_en: The water of the channel turns the great mill.
notes: Gheada 'rego'/'gran' [reho/hɾaŋ]; palatal nasal 'muíño'; word-initial trill [r] 'rego' per fernandez_rei1990.
